sourceMovie.title: The Black Dahlia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Black Dahlia for the same reason it broke through in 2006: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Brian De Palma keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kiss the Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Black Dahlia clicked for you, Kiss the Girls (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Black Dahlia?
faq.answer: Try Kiss the Girls, Death Wish II, The Long Good Friday—each shares DNA with The Black Dahlia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Black Dahlia?
faq.answer: The Black Dahlia (2006) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Black Dahlia worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
